title: The Fresh Prince Project

jokes.text: Will mistakes Geoffrey the butler for Uncle Phil
jokes.setup: Will enthusiastically greets who he thinks is his uncle
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's formal correction reveals Will's mistake
jokes.explanation: Strong character-establishing joke that shows Will's culture clash immediately. Well-structured misdirection.

jokes.text: I didn't know there were so many brothers living in this neighborhood
jokes.setup: Will assumes Geoffrey is a neighbor rather than staff
jokes.punchline: The casual assumption reveals Will's unfamiliarity with wealthy households
jokes.explanation: Efficient character work showing Will's street perspective clashing with Bel-Air reality.

jokes.text: Okay, well, I... Cheerio and all that rot. Bring the horses round, would you?
jokes.setup: Will learns Geoffrey is the butler
jokes.punchline: Will immediately affects an exaggerated British accent and aristocratic mannerisms
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Will's adaptability and humor. The 'horses' bit is particularly absurd and memorable.

jokes.text: Who are you, Robo-butler, man?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's overly formal speech about household traditions
jokes.punchline: Will's nickname combining 'robot' and 'butler' to describe Geoffrey's rigidity
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau that perfectly captures Geoffrey's character through Will's street-smart perspective.

jokes.text: Make it sound like we're back on the plantation, like: 'Massa William! Massa William!'
jokes.setup: Will objects to being called 'Master William'
jokes.punchline: Direct reference to slavery with exaggerated plantation accent
jokes.explanation: Bold subversive humor that addresses racial dynamics head-on while maintaining comedy. Risky but earned through character authenticity.

jokes.text: Check this: 'His Royal Freshness.' That's dope.
jokes.setup: Will wants an alternative to 'Master William'
jokes.punchline: Suggests the absurdly grandiose 'His Royal Freshness'
jokes.explanation: Perfect blend of Will's ego and the show's title. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Geoffrey says 'Master William, walk this way' and Will responds 'No.'
jokes.setup: Geoffrey gives a formal directive
jokes.punchline: Will's flat refusal breaks the formal rhythm
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective comedic beat. The economy is perfect - one word delivers the rebellion.

jokes.text: That was the plan.
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv comments on how Will has grown into a man
jokes.punchline: Will's matter-of-fact response treats normal aging as intentional strategy
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery that shows Will's confidence without being obnoxious.

jokes.text: The plane ride translation confusion - 'stupid' to 'dope' to 'neat'
jokes.setup: Will uses street slang 'stupid' meaning good, Aunt Viv doesn't understand
jokes.punchline: The escalating translation attempts ending with the whitest possible word 'neat'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating structure showing the culture gap. The progression from 'stupid' to 'neat' is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: You got to love this guy, right?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's continued formality despite Will's attempts at casual connection
jokes.punchline: Will's rhetorical question to the audience about Geoffrey's rigid behavior
jokes.explanation: Functional audience connection moment but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: That's stupid. / Now he's got you doing it.
jokes.setup: Vivian uses 'stupid' in response to Philip
jokes.punchline: Philip points out she's now using the confusing slang
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback structure showing Will's influence spreading. Clean setup-punchline execution.

jokes.text: Next time I go to my room, I'm gonna take some bread crumbs.
jokes.setup: Will comments on how huge the house is
jokes.punchline: References Hansel and Gretel fairy tale to navigate the mansion
jokes.explanation: Clever fairy tale reference that perfectly captures the culture shock. Highly quotable and visual.

jokes.text: Hi. My little Scottish cousin.
jokes.setup: Will meets Ashley for the first time
jokes.punchline: Describes her as Scottish based on her posh accent/mannerisms
jokes.explanation: Decent observation about Ashley's refined speech patterns from Will's perspective.

jokes.text: Don't you just hate it when that happens? / Yes, that is my pet peeve.
jokes.setup: Ashley mentions pool heater being broken
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic 'first world problem' response, Ashley's earnest agreement
jokes.explanation: Good class consciousness humor showing the wealth gap through Ashley's innocent response.

jokes.text: Yo, this is better than Love Boat. This boy gonna be maxing and relaxing.
jokes.setup: Will learns about pool and tennis court
jokes.punchline: References TV show and uses slang to express his excitement
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Will's excitement, though the pop culture reference is somewhat dated.

jokes.text: Hi. Dad, I need $300. / Hi. Dad, I need $300.
jokes.setup: Hilary enters the scene
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks for money, ignoring introductions, then repeats it
jokes.explanation: Perfect character introduction establishing Hilary's self-absorption and materialism.

jokes.text: For what? / Probably her head.
jokes.setup: Philip asks what Hilary needs a hat for
jokes.punchline: Will's literal, deadpan response
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. Simple but effective structure with great economy.

jokes.text: Look, if this weren't a good idea, I really don't think Ally Sheedy would be involved.
jokes.setup: Will points out the logical contradiction in the environmental plan
jokes.punchline: Hilary's appeal to celebrity authority as definitive proof
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of celebrity worship and shallow thinking. Perfect character logic for Hilary.

jokes.text: Hey, look who's here. Benson.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey appears with Will's tuxedo
jokes.punchline: Will references the TV show 'Benson' about a black butler
jokes.explanation: Smart pop culture reference that comments on the racial dynamics of the butler character.

jokes.text: But I didn't get you anything.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey says 'This is for you' referring to the tuxedo
jokes.punchline: Will interprets it as a gift exchange situation
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection showing Will's unfamiliarity with formal service dynamics.

jokes.text: Yo, are we having a party? We're gonna get stupid, right?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey mentions the dinner with law partners
jokes.punchline: Will's excitement about 'getting stupid' at a formal dinner
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the slang theme showing Will's misunderstanding of the event's formality.

jokes.text: For some of us, that will require very little effort indeed.
jokes.setup: Will says they're gonna get stupid at the party
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry insult suggesting Will's already stupid
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey moment - sophisticated insult delivered with perfect butler composure. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: That's all you had to say to get rid of him? I've been racking my brain.
jokes.setup: Ashley dismisses Geoffrey with a simple 'That will be all'
jokes.punchline: Will's amazement at how easy it was to dismiss the butler
jokes.explanation: Shows Will learning the household dynamics while highlighting his previous struggles with Geoffrey.

jokes.text: Not deaf. D-E-F. That's just slang. Means terrific, good.
jokes.setup: Ashley thinks Will called her 'deaf'
jokes.punchline: Will explains 'def' is slang for good, continuing the slang confusion theme
jokes.explanation: Continues the established slang theme, though this particular instance is less clever than earlier ones.

jokes.text: The rap battle with Ashley about the chorus audition
jokes.setup: Will teaches Ashley to rap about her chorus rejection
jokes.punchline: Their collaborative rap about hitting the teacher with a thesaurus
jokes.explanation: Creative sequence showing Will's positive influence on Ashley, blending education and street culture.

jokes.text: When you two were little, people thought you were twins. You looked exactly alike. / I guess some things never change.
jokes.setup: Vivian says Will and Carlton looked identical as children
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic response implies they still look nothing alike
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the visual contrast between Will and Carlton, delivered with perfect timing.

jokes.text: And Bryant Gumbel. He's darn good.
jokes.setup: Will asks who Carlton's heroes are
jokes.punchline: Carlton lists Bryant Gumbel as a hero alongside his father
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - choosing the most milquetoast TV personality as a hero. Brilliantly specific choice.

jokes.text: Other than that thing at Buckingham Palace, no.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks if Will has attended formal dinners
jokes.punchline: Will claims casual experience with British royalty
jokes.explanation: Great Will moment - answering honestly while bragging absurdly. Perfect character confidence.

jokes.text: Yes. It's definitely the cat's meow.
jokes.setup: Carlton uses outdated slang 'neat'
jokes.punchline: Will responds with even more antiquated slang 'cat's meow'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the outdated slang theme, showing Will adapting to Carlton's style.

jokes.text: I don't think you have to worry about anybody mistaking you for a brother.
jokes.setup: Carlton thinks they might be mistaken for brothers in tuxedos
jokes.punchline: Will's double entendre about Carlton not seeming 'black enough'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double entendre that addresses racial identity with perfect comedic timing. Establishes a key show dynamic.

jokes.text: Touché.
jokes.setup: Will's comment about Carlton not being mistaken for a 'brother'
jokes.punchline: Carlton's sophisticated acknowledgment of being burned
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton response - even when insulted, he responds with sophistication rather than anger.

jokes.text: Not Will. President Reagan.
jokes.setup: Parents ask 'Where is he?' seemingly about Will
jokes.punchline: Hilary reveals she's asking about President Reagan
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing Hilary's celebrity obsession and privilege.

jokes.text: He's turned down the last 16 invitations. When are you gonna take a hint?
jokes.setup: Philip wants to invite Reagan again
jokes.punchline: The absurd number of rejections reveals Philip's persistence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke showing Philip's social climbing through specific numbers.

jokes.text: Where can I send a donation? / I don't know.
jokes.setup: Guest is moved by Hilary's environmental passion speech
jokes.punchline: Hilary admits she doesn't know how to actually help
jokes.explanation: Perfect puncturing of Hilary's performative environmentalism. Great structure and character consistency.

jokes.text: Earth, Wind & Fire! When's your next album coming out?
jokes.setup: Philip introduces law firm 'Furth, Winn and Meyer'
jokes.punchline: Will hears it as the band 'Earth, Wind & Fire'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that's both character-appropriate and genuinely clever. The phonetic similarity is perfect.

jokes.text: Really? How much do you think we could get for that stereo?
jokes.setup: Guest mentions he used to 'fence' at Bel-Air Academy
jokes.punchline: Will interprets 'fence' as selling stolen goods, not the sport
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing Will's street background versus upper-class activities. Perfect character logic.

jokes.text: How could you do that? / It's all in the tongue. I'll show you later.
jokes.setup: Carlton presumably asks about Will's eating technique
jokes.punchline: Will's response suggests something inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Decent innuendo joke that fits Will's character but isn't particularly sophisticated.

jokes.text: Any requests?
jokes.setup: Philip says there are other people at the table (presumably complaining about Will's behavior)
jokes.punchline: Will treats it like he's a dinner entertainer taking requests
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's complete misreading of the formal dinner situation with characteristic confidence.

jokes.text: Ashley's grace rap ending with 'Thank you, God, for this stupid food'
jokes.setup: Ashley is asked to say grace
jokes.punchline: She delivers it as a rap, ending with Will's slang usage
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff combining multiple established elements. Shows Will's positive influence while shocking the adults.

jokes.text: I officially go off duty at 9:00 p.m. And if you care to look at your watch, you will note that the long hand is on 12, and the short hand is on 9. It is 9:00, Master William.
jokes.setup: Will asks Geoffrey to get him cocoa
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's elaborate explanation of being off duty with condescending clock-reading lesson
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - formal language used for petty revenge. The clock explanation is beautifully condescending.

jokes.text: And do you know what that means? / Masterpiece Theatre is on.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asks if Will knows what 9 PM means
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's priority is watching highbrow television
jokes.explanation: Great character detail showing Geoffrey's pretensions and priorities. Perfectly delivered punchline.

jokes.text: Man, I was with you up till 'skewering.'
jokes.setup: Philip delivers an elaborate lecture with sophisticated vocabulary
jokes.punchline: Will admits he lost track at a specific big word
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke that acknowledges the audience's likely confusion while staying true to character.

jokes.text: You actually heard Malcolm speak, man?
jokes.setup: Philip reveals he heard Malcolm X speak in person
jokes.punchline: Will's surprised respect, dropping his defensive attitude
jokes.explanation: Important character moment showing Will's genuine respect for his hero, creating connection with Philip.

jokes.text: He really likes the Care Bears.
jokes.setup: Will asks what music Philip approves of for Ashley
jokes.punchline: Philip's musical taste for his daughter is extremely juvenile
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Philip's overprotectiveness through absurdly safe music choice.

jokes.text: It's that he's from Mars, Ashley.
jokes.setup: Will explains why Philip does seemingly irrational things
jokes.punchline: After a thoughtful buildup, Will concludes Philip is an alien
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - starts serious and philosophical, ends with absurd explanation. Perfect Will logic.

jokes.text: You're like the big brother I never had. / What are you talking about? You got... You're right. I see your point.
jokes.setup: Ashley says Will is like the big brother she never had
jokes.punchline: Will starts to contradict her then realizes Carlton doesn't count as a 'big brother'
jokes.explanation: Great callback that pays off the Carlton characterization while showing Will's self-awareness.

jokes.text: A half bath? How does that work? / You better show me a full bathroom. I don't want to take no chances.
jokes.setup: Ashley mentions they have four and a half bathrooms
jokes.punchline: Will's literal interpretation and practical concern about bathroom functionality
jokes.explanation: Good practical humor showing Will's unfamiliarity with luxury home terminology.

jokes.text: Can't a person gargle in peace?
jokes.setup: Will knocks on bathroom door while Hilary is inside
jokes.punchline: Hilary's dramatic complaint about gargling privacy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary - treating basic hygiene like sacred ritual. Character-specific vanity and drama.

jokes.text: I knew it!
jokes.setup: Extended sequence of Will trying different bathrooms
jokes.punchline: Will's triumphant exclamation upon presumably finding the right bathroom
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that provides closure to the bathroom running gag, though not particularly clever.

tmdb_overview: Aunt Vivian tries to mediate between her husband Philip and the newly arrived Will.
